mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to equity cash flows, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate negative equity repricing over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances, or signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of final goods and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 2% annualized during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth >2% during tightening yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of observations, or regime-specific IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with declining nonfarm payrolls creates stagflationary pressure that forces immediate equity sell-offs over the next week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases and PAYEMS declines month-over-month
failure_condition: Concurrent rate hikes and payroll declines produce positive 5-day returns in >55% of instances, or interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter regardless of monetary policy stance.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of quarters, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding 3.5% annually triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL > 3.5% yields positive 20-day returns in >60% of triggered months, or threshold signal shows no out-of-sample predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and consumer spending power, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
